author: Vinayak S.
pros: Very user friendly. Comes with a mobile app which greatly helped me watch my university lecture videos on the go. Option to download video as well as you can put a restriction such that a user cannot download.
cons: Sometimes the videos take a long time to buffer. The quality of video does not auto adjust with the internet speed quickly.

review_title: Solid, reliable and configurable monitoring and alerting solution
description: We started using Panopta over a year ago after having continual problems with some of the other better-known monitoring solutions; continual false-negative reports and convoluted interfaces were causing continual problems for us. Panopta's service has proven to be very reliable, allowing us to configure the points from which our servers are monitored which mean we're getting alerts from regions very close to our users, and we've been able to extend this past simple 'ping' type tests. Their alerting services are easily configurable and adaptable, meaning our staff receives alerts promptly when necessary. We have also had no issues with simple integrations via API to any services we've wanted so far. A couple of times we haven't been able to figure out issues on our own their support has proven to be very fast to respond and diligent with following up on any lingering concerns. Happy to recommend Panopta to anyone looking for a reliable, robust monitoring solution.
author: Rafe H.
pros: Ease of use; reliability; configurability; alerting systems; easily integrated with other systems
cons: They don't have as many 'out of the box' integrations as some of the better-known solutions, however, it hasn't proven to be a big hurdle with a little work.

description: Overall we're happy - all outages seem to be notified. The issue we do have is false notifications, that tend to wake up engineers in the middle of the night for no reason.
author: Karl H.

review_title: Basic Network Monitoring Platform for startups
description: not so satisfied but I think the amount of money we pay for Panopta is just fair to the benefits we receive
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I like about Panopta is it is very easy to setup. The other thing is that it has built-in SMS notification system which can be used when there are critical alerts.
cons: Panopta lacks SNMP polling. It is limited to ICMP monitoring

review_title: Panopta is a critical element of any hosting setup
description: I have run a boutique hosting business alongside my web-based app development business for 17 years now. It works out well to have one company develop the software and host it. Being a small shop, I use outside services extensively to put my service offering together, and Panopta is a big part of that. I have never had problems with Panopta, it's always there when I need it. I could easily put together a system myself to monitor my websites. However, Panopta has a great feature that is difficult to replicate cheaply. Your website has a primary monitoring location - say "California". If the primary monitoring location finds your site down or unresponsive, it alerts other monitoring locations around the world. They all try to hit it then and only then determine if it's down. So if a connection on the internet is down but my site is up, Panopta won't bug me. I've never had a false alarm from Panopta. I was an early customer from what I can tell (I remember the founder emailing me directly once when I asked a question) and will be with them for a long time. The service has matured nicely over the years and really serves an important role in my company.
author: Michael C.
cons: Sometimes difficult to setup, but the complexity is expected for the service provided.

description: I switched from Pingdom after they made "improvements" that rendered a good product unusable. Switching to Panopta was easy, and gave me some extra flexibility in composing checks across multiple protocols. After two years I have not missed a single outage thanks to Panopta, the service is consistent and reliable. My main complaint is that their configuration UI is somewhat clunky and slow. Multiple page reloads are often performed for simple updates, and this can make it very slow to make bulk changes. A rewrite using up-to-date single-page techniques would make the UI experience much more fluid.
author: Richard L.
cons: Config UI is slow and clunky.

description: I was first attracted by their brilliant name and tag line. Looking into the service I was quite impressed at how it operated. We immediately signed up and use it to monitor a few handfuls of nodes. We have now used it for a few years and love it. Our only gripe, and it's more my personal expectations, but faster email response and a reseller or affiliate program :)
author: Keith P.
cons: Support doesn't reply as fast as cPanel. Possibly just my unrealistic 3-hour expectations though.

description: Our servers went down before and after we signed up for Panopta's monitoring service. The difference is that we can act on it immediately now. It took literally 2 minutes to setup the service once we had signed up. The wizard did all the work. Now we get texts and emails within a minute of anything happening. This kind of peace of mind is priceless.
author: Kim P.
pros: Easy to setup. Can monitor just about anything we would need.
cons: Have not experienced anything negative yet on Panopta. (Been using it for over 2 years)

review_title: Just the right amount of monitoring
description: We use Panopta as our front-line monitoring system at Nutshell, a SaaS product. In particular, we needed a tool that would notify the team via phone call, for late-night coverage. Panopta made this easy. We struggled with the iPhone app at first, but they've launched a new version which works great for us. It's nice to get an iPhone notification as a heads-up before we escalate to phone/text. The UI to attach notification schedules to services is a bit unwieldy, but they've made significant progress. We lean toward a simple configuration, ("Is the app up?"), and put members into several escalation groups (iPhone -> text -> phone). The effort, cost, and overhead of PagerDuty haven't proven necessary. Panopta's actual core monitoring platform has been bulletproof for us: no false positives, immediate notifications. It helpfully covers things like SMTP, DNS, and SSL expiry, too. We use other monitoring platforms, but Panopta is the source of truth, for both the operations team and the rest of the company!
author: Andy F.
pros: Phone call alerting, bulletproof monitoring, straightforward monitoring of an HTTPS endpoint
cons: The UI to add a new team member to an existing schedule is a bit kludgy

review_title: Quick setup and relatively easy monitorying
description: Overall they have been great to work with
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Panopta’s support was very helpful in getting us up and running. So far it has been able to scale with our infrastrutures and is very user friendly when it comes to monitoring.
cons: We've had some false positive alerts, but haven't had those in a while so we think it was a bug that they fixed or we didn't have it configured properly out the gate.
author_profession: Adjunct Faculty - College of Business & Economics

review_title: I used Panopta to manage our multiple data centers.
author: Forrest D.
pros: I like the flexibility of monitoring. The software that I used previously was computer based and prone to crashing. Having a web based solution is much more practical and stable.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I like the service once setup properly. Something lacking is good java/jmx monitoring, not easy to read and no heap/garbage collection metrics I can see. The UI needs to be revamped I think, more ajax calls etc.
author: eamonn M.
cons: More advaced metrics like java tomcat metrics. The ui is not too user friendly

review_title: Panopta gives me peace of mind
description: Our small firm uses Panopta to monitor critical services on our client and collocated servers. Over the years we've used in-house, self-hosted or other third party solutions. None have offered the features, value, ease of use or reliability of Panopta. They are quick to respond to any questions and are now an integral part of my business.
author: Andrew C.

description: We made a good choice with Panopta. For a very reasonable annual fee, I am able to keep an eye on server health, easily configure uptime and content monitors, and customize my alerts pretty easily. I've had responsive support and can see the developers continue to invest in the product.
author: Joseph W.

description: I setup Panopta to monitor the uptime of my servers to make sure we had little to no downtime. Any questions or issues that arose, Panopta support team was there to help. The control panel configuration setup was also a seamless process.
author: Shabbir M.

description: The Panopta software does what is says on the box. We manage all our customer systems using Panopta and know immediately if anything needs attention via email alerts. This service is invaluable to us.
author: Robin A.

description: Good, powerful monitoring service, like how you can set thresholds for the alerts, would be good if you could 'force' a retest at any time but otherwise very happy with Panopta.
author: David M.

description: Although we're new to Panopta, it's been a huge leap over the products we've used in years past. The price can't be beat and it does exactly what we want it to do.
author: Mike R.

description: I have used Panopta for 4 years as a way of externally monitoring my company websites and perimeter hardware. It has worked quite well.
author: Christopher C.

review_title: Really is always on the lookout
description: Panopta is a great monitoring tool. Slightly more advanced than others on the market which suits our technical requirements.
author: John B.

description: Panopta has been wonderful for providing server monitoring for our applications. The ability to add custom dashboards to show key metrics and add alerts when those metrics are out of policy has been key to monitoring our physical infrastructure and has allowed us to focus on running our products vs running our systems. I would highly recommend to anyone looking to monitor public/private cloud and physical systems.
author: Jonathan D.
pros: - Cheaper than competitors - More features than competitors of similar price - Very robust dashboard an alert system
cons: - Dashboards can be a bit difficult to change - Not all features are obvious and can take some getting used to

description: It's hard enough to keep up with servers 24/7/365 let alone when I'm traveling. The website and app make outages easy to manage when I'm on the go. The alerts come right to my phone. For the price this may be the best vendor I have. Scratch that, "IS"! The price is the same as when I signed up yet now I have a public status page and agent heartbeat to keep my business ticket. It's alive and well. Thanks Panopta!
author: Seth C.
pros: Agent heartbeat has a very light footprint on the servers. The flexibility of outage alerts is easy to customize as well.
cons: Once, in a while I'll get a false alarm. Although, it's typically due to a new monitoring IP that needs whitelisting. Not too much trouble at all.

description: We have been using Panopta for a couple years now and find it extremely useful, we have a network of sites that we need to monitor for uptime and we thoroughly depend on Panopta now to monitor their uptime, Panopta has alerted us instantly when the sites or servers are down and helped our team identify issues when a resource is down right away. I would recommend this to anyone is need for a monitoring resource.
author: Mihir S.

review_title: An app to record your teaching for class
description: The cloud Panopta provides to upload the recoded lectures is very useful for online teaching. Our school used Panopta and connected it to the school teaching website so students can listen to lectures from the school website using Panopta. Overall, it comes with many good features necessary for online teaching.
author: Amin G.
pros: It is basically an app that you can use to upload your class explanation and use then recording as an online source. Panopta has this nice feature to cut and edit the videos very easily which is very important for many teachers who are not expert in editing videos.
cons: The recording quality is not that good. I tried couple of time to record my lecture but I had to be very careful about any little noise since it was transformed to the voice. So having a very good quality microphone is very important. Often I had to use other platforms to record my lecture and upload it on Panopta to share with students.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Powerful Intuitive Website Monitoring - Just Lacks a Couple of Features
description: I love Panopta. It is my favorite platform for just monitoring websites. The outage simulator, easy tree-style interface, and awesome support will always make it a great system to work with. Just needs a few extra features such a RUM, APM, etc. There are other competitors that offer similar services for similar prices while also offering additional features as part of the over package. But, competitor's support is lacking as well as ease of use.
author: Kevin W.
pros: - Nested Object Interface - On-Call Schedule Built-In - Calling for Free - Large amount of monitor types - Large amount of locations.
cons: - No RUM - No Docker - No APM
